items.title: Betting Against Wall Street: The Inverse ETF Surge No One Is Talking About
items.author: Todd Shriber
items.description: The ProShares UltraShort Financials ETF (SKF) is gaining attention as a bearish bet on financial stocks, offering -2x daily leverage against the S&P Financial Select Sector index. While some investors remain skittish about banks due to memories of the 2008 financial crisis, the article cautions that leveraged inverse ETFs are only suitable for short-term tactical trading, not long-term holdings. The fund can serve as a hedge against major financial stocks like Berkshire Hathaway and JPMorgan Chase.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article presents the ETF as a tactical tool with both potential benefits and significant risks. While it can serve as a short-term hedge, the author emphasizes caution and warns against long-term holding, indicating a balanced but cautious view.

items.title: JPMorgan Acknowledges Shutting Down Trump Accounts After January 6
items.description: JPMorgan Chase & Co. (NYSE: JPM) acknowledged in a court filing that it shut down bank accounts tied to President Donald Trump and several of his businesses after the Jan. 6, 2021, Capitol riot, a point now sitting at the center of Trump's $5 billion lawsuit against the lender and its chief executive.

items.title: Goldman’s board kills DEI — and that’s not a terrible thing
items.description: Corporate boards are overwhelmingly composed of former executives and financial professionals. Is that really diversity?

items.title: Trump's Global Tariffs, GDP Slump, Recession Warning And More: This Week In Economy
items.description: The week had major economic and political events, including a Supreme Court ruling on trade authority and a recession warning.
